body: [{"ac":1,"text":"A bead whose primary route fails mid-execution with provider_connectivity retries on the next route in the readiness fallback chain at the same power class.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":2,"text":"If all same-power alternates fail with provider_connectivity, escalate power class.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":3,"text":"If all power classes exhaust with the same connectivity failure pattern, mark the bead failed.","kind":"prose","verifiable":false},{"ac":4,"text":"Reproduce: simulate bragi/sindri local-endpoint connection drop during do-phase and observe automatic fallback to claude/opus without operator intervention.","kind":"negative","verifiable":true}]

status=proposed
reason=ac-quality:needs-refinement — fix requires design decision and verified repro before code change Investigation summary: The bead's "likely fix" (distinguish escalation-exhausted from same-power-alternates-exhausted) names a real seam, but the implementation choice is non-obvious and the empirical gap is not fully explained by an obvious read of current code. Operator design input is needed before committing routing/escalation changes. Relevant code paths examined in this worktree: 1. cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:299 — runEscalatingPowerAttempts short-circuits on report.Disrupted=true and never retries at the same power. provider_connectivity sets Disrupted=true (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2715-2718), so the in-execution path never attempts the alternate at the same power class. 2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4798 — applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion calls nextFloorFn(report.ActualPower) which only inspects power classes STRICTLY > actualPower (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go:97). When the failed route is at the top power and same-power alternates exist (e.g. claude/opus at the same power class as bragi/qwen3.6-27b-autoround), the ladder emits kind=execution-escalation-aborted at power N even though a viable alternate remains. 3. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4733 — CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions (called on the NEXT claim, cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:449) builds ExcludedRoutes from the failed-route list and re-runs ResolveRoute at the same minPower. In theory this should pick up the alternate on the next iteration; in the user's repro, it did not. Root cause for that gap is not visible from static read of the code (could be Available/AutoRoutable catalog state, worker iteration ordering, or a pausedInfraUntil interaction) and requires reproduction before fixing. 4. AC4 ("simulate bragi/sindri local-endpoint connection drop during do-phase and observe automatic fallback to claude/opus") needs integration-level scaffolding that doesn't exist for this code path. The existing tests at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go cover unit-level behavior of applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion but cannot simulate a real mid-execution drop end-to-end. Open design questions for operator: - Should the in-execution executor retry on the alternate at the same power class (modify runEscalatingPowerAttempts to track session-local failed routes and reject Disrupted-as-terminal), OR should it remain "next claim retries via failed_routes exclusion" (and we instead fix whatever blocked the empirical retry)? - Should applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion be made aware of same-power alternates (consult a resolveRoute callback) so it suppresses the misleading escalation-aborted event when alternates remain? - What is the test scaffolding strategy for AC4? Stub fizeau ResolveRoute that returns bragi on first call and claude/opus on second, and exercise the worker loop end-to-end? Or a tighter unit test that asserts the alternate is consulted? Suggested decomposition once design is settled: (a) Repro test: extend route-exclusion tests to assert the failed bead is reclaimed and dispatched on the alternate within the same drain iteration. (b) Implementation: route-exclusion path consults same-power viability before emitting escalation-aborted; OR in-executor retry on Disrupted+connectivity. (c) Integration: end-to-end worker test with stubbed provider drop mid-execution. No code changes attempted in this attempt — committing speculative escalation logic without a verified repro risks regressing existing routing behavior.

rationale: status: proposed
reason: ac-quality:needs-refinement — fix requires design decision and verified repro before code change

Investigation summary:

The bead's "likely fix" (distinguish escalation-exhausted from same-power-alternates-exhausted) names a real seam, but the implementation choice is non-obvious and the empirical gap is not fully explained by an obvious read of current code. Operator design input is needed before committing routing/escalation changes.

Relevant code paths examined in this worktree:

1. cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop_escalation.go:299 — runEscalatingPowerAttempts short-circuits on report.Disrupted=true and never retries at the same power. provider_connectivity sets Disrupted=true (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:2715-2718), so the in-execution path never attempts the alternate at the same power class.

2. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4798 — applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion calls nextFloorFn(report.ActualPower) which only inspects power classes STRICTLY > actualPower (cli/internal/agent/escalation/ladder.go:97). When the failed route is at the top power and same-power alternates exist (e.g. claude/opus at the same power class as bragi/qwen3.6-27b-autoround), the ladder emits kind=execution-escalation-aborted at power N even though a viable alternate remains.

3. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4733 — CheckAndApplyRouteExclusions (called on the NEXT claim, cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:449) builds ExcludedRoutes from the failed-route list and re-runs ResolveRoute at the same minPower. In theory this should pick up the alternate on the next iteration; in the user's repro, it did not. Root cause for that gap is not visible from static read of the code (could be Available/AutoRoutable catalog state, worker iteration ordering, or a pausedInfraUntil interaction) and requires reproduction before fixing.

4. AC4 ("simulate bragi/sindri local-endpoint connection drop during do-phase and observe automatic fallback to claude/opus") needs integration-level scaffolding that doesn't exist for this code path. The existing tests at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_escalation_aborted_test.go cover unit-level behavior of applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion but cannot simulate a real mid-execution drop end-to-end.

Open design questions for operator:

- Should the in-execution executor retry on the alternate at the same power class (modify runEscalatingPowerAttempts to track session-local failed routes and reject Disrupted-as-terminal), OR should it remain "next claim retries via failed_routes exclusion" (and we instead fix whatever blocked the empirical retry)?

- Should applyProviderConnectivityRouteExclusion be made aware of same-power alternates (consult a resolveRoute callback) so it suppresses the misleading escalation-aborted event when alternates remain?

- What is the test scaffolding strategy for AC4? Stub fizeau ResolveRoute that returns bragi on first call and claude/opus on second, and exercise the worker loop end-to-end? Or a tighter unit test that asserts the alternate is consulted?

Suggested decomposition once design is settled:

  (a) Repro test: extend route-exclusion tests to assert the failed bead is reclaimed and dispatched on the alternate within the same drain iteration.
  (b) Implementation: route-exclusion path consults same-power viability before emitting escalation-aborted; OR in-executor retry on Disrupted+connectivity.
  (c) Integration: end-to-end worker test with stubbed provider drop mid-execution.

No code changes attempted in this attempt — committing speculative escalation logic without a verified repro risks regressing existing routing behavior.
